sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method provenance
Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰), dual descriptor, and identification of the most electrophilic (f⁺) and nucleophilic (f⁻) atomic sites for nitrobenzene. Headline numerical value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI approximation (density fitting) in PySCF 2.5.0.
Geometry: fixed user-supplied nitrobenzene.xyz (no further optimisation); single-point calculations on neutral (N), cation (N+1, doublet), and anion (N−1, doublet) states – i.e. DFT//exp-geom protocol.
Solvent: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge/multiplicity: 0 / 1 (neutral), +1 / 2 (cation), –1 / 2 (anion).
Convergence: SCF converged for all three charge states. Engine integrity verdict: trustworthy.
Warnings (verbatim):
 – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
 – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
 – common header present
Reproducibility: live log was written to `fukui_20260703-195927.out` (still available); full command recorded in the JSON. Output files: `nitrobenzene_fukui.json`, accompanying plot image.

Key results
• Maximum electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ = 0.4964178 on atom 4 (1-based index, C atom).
• Maximum nucleophilic Fukui index f⁻ = 0.1072882 on atom 5 (C atom).
• Dual descriptor highlights atom 4 as the primary electrophilic site (Δf = 0.51070984).

Computed data should be interpreted qualitatively; Fukui indices depend on method, basis, and charge partitioning scheme.
